message:
fix(fcp-bootstrap): hand recovery phrase back to caller via BootstrapOutcome (br-sy5ks)

Closes flywheel_connectors-sy5ks (P1 identity-recovery).

Reviewer found a silent catastrophic UX failure in single-device
bootstrap. workflow.rs:399 generated a RecoveryPhrase, logged only
'Recovery phrase generated. Store it securely!' (the actual words
were NEVER emitted), fed the phrase to a tracing::debug no-op
save_recovery_phrase at :619 ('Recovery phrase would be saved
(encrypted) to secure storage'), then silently dropped it. Net
effect: a 'successful' ceremony left the operator with NO phrase
in-hand and NO persisted copy. Total device loss = total owner
identity loss with no recovery path.

AGENTS.md forbids plaintext secrets on disk, so the fix deliberately
does not persist. Instead, the library now hands the phrase back to
the caller exactly once via a new BootstrapOutcome return type:

    #[must_use]
    pub struct BootstrapOutcome {
        pub genesis: GenesisState,
        pub recovery_phrase: Option<RecoveryPhrase>,
    }

Invariants pinned by regression tests:
- SingleDevice mode: recovery_phrase == Some(phrase), and
  phrase.derive_owner_keypair().public() MUST equal
  genesis.owner_public_key (the phrase handed out is the one that
  actually created the genesis, not a decoy).
- MultiDevice mode: recovery_phrase == None (FROST shards ARE the
  recovery).
- Import mode: recovery_phrase == None (caller already has the
  canonical phrase; re-emitting would be a leak).
- HardwareToken mode: recovery_phrase == None (key lives on token).

Deleted save_recovery_phrase entirely — the debug no-op was misleading
both the operator and the security audit trail. A refactor-defense
comment points at fcp_crypto::shamir for future split-and-distribute
persistence if needed.

#[must_use] on BootstrapOutcome + explicit BootstrapOutcome.into_genesis()
helper make the discard of recovery_phrase visible in code review: a
single-device caller that writes .into_genesis() is saying 'I
deliberately chose not to show the phrase'. Deref<Target=GenesisState>
was deliberately NOT implemented — it would have hidden the discard.

RecoveryPhrase remains ZeroizeOnDrop so a caller that accepts the
outcome but never reads recovery_phrase still wipes the bytes on drop.
Safe default; bad UX; the regression test prevents accidental
regression on the library side.

Migrated ~15 test call sites to .run().unwrap().into_genesis() or
outcome.genesis pattern. No production callers outside this crate
(grep confirmed); fwc does not yet drive BootstrapWorkflow directly.

Three regression tests pin the invariants:
- single_device_bootstrap_outcome_carries_recovery_phrase (+ verifies
  the phrase derives the genesis's owner_public_key)
- multi_device_bootstrap_outcome_carries_no_recovery_phrase
- import_bootstrap_outcome_does_not_re_emit_phrase

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-bootstrap — 807/807 pass (714 lib + 23 +
9 + 61 integration), zero regression.

Cc_1 / SunnyMoose IMPLEMENTER
